channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia and Ukraine exchange 205 prisoners each in latest swap
item.description: The prisoner exchange marks a continued diplomatic effort despite ongoing hostilities, with both sides bringing home service personnel from Russian and Ukrainian captivity.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Zelenskiy warns Russia may be planning NATO attack from Belarus
item.description: Ukraine's president says intelligence suggests Russia is considering military operations against a NATO country using Belarusian territory, raising concerns about escalation.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Drone warfare becomes deadliest threat to Sudanese civilians
item.description: Armed drones account for over 80% of conflict-related civilian deaths in Sudan, with at least 880 civilians killed by drone strikes between January and April 2026.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan presses US for arms sales after Trump signals uncertainty
item.description: Following Trump's comments during his China visit expressing hesitation about defending Taiwan, the island's government emphasizes the importance of US arms deals for regional security.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US and Nigerian forces kill ISIS second-in-command, Trump announces
item.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described as the global second-in-command of ISIS, was eliminated in a joint US-Nigerian military operation, according to President Trump.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 Trump Leaves China With Warm Rhetoric But Few Concrete Results
item.description: Trump departed Beijing after a summit rich in pageantry but offering little tangible progress on Iran, Taiwan, or AI. Xi warned of potential conflict over Taiwan while Trump remained noncommittal on arms sales. No major breakthroughs emerged on the key issues dividing the two superpowers.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇨🇳 Putin to Visit China Soon as Kremlin Announces Completed Preparations
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will visit Beijing very soon, the Kremlin said, with preparations complete. The visit comes days after Trump-Xi summit, raising speculation about triangular diplomacy between US, China, and Russia.

item.title: ⚖️🇺🇦🇪🇺 36 Countries and EU Approve Special Tribunal to Prosecute Putin for Aggression
item.description: A historic legal agreement creates a tribunal to prosecute Russia's top leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine, closing a legal loophole that the ICC could not address. Zelensky called it a shield for the future while standing at the site of a Kyiv residential strike that killed 24.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇵🇭⚔️ Japan Considers Missile Exports to Philippines in Historic Defense Shift
item.description: Tokyo last month unveiled its biggest overhaul of defense export rules in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms transfers. Japan may now export missiles to the Philippines, marking a significant shift from its pacifist post-war defense posture.

item.title: 🇲🇲💥🛢️ Myanmar Civil War Compounded by Distant Iran Conflict
item.description: Farmers in Myanmar face fuel and fertilizer shortages as ships are diverted away from the Strait of Hormuz, hitting agricultural supply chains. The Iran conflict 2,000 miles away is exacerbating hardships in a country already ravaged by civil war since the 2021 coup.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskiy Vows Retribution After Russian Missile Strike Kills 24 in Kyiv
item.description: President Zelenskiy promised retribution after a Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment building killed 24 people including three children, vowing justice for the victims.

item.title: 🌍 BRICS Fails to Issue Joint Statement Over Iran War Divisions
item.description: Top diplomats from BRICS nations failed to issue a joint statement after a meeting in Delhi, exposing divisions over the Iran conflict. Host India released only a chair statement.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Says Losing Patience With Iran, Did Not Ask China for Favors
item.description: President Trump said his patience with Iran is running out and that Xi Jinping agreed Tehran must reopen the Strait of Hormuz, though China gave no indication it would intervene.

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvia government collapses over Ukrainian drone controversy
item.description: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after her coalition fell apart following disputes over handling stray Ukrainian drones that crossed into Latvian airspace from Russia, raising questions about NATO border defense readiness.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin declares Ukraine conflict coming to an end after scaled-back Victory Day
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested the war in Ukraine could be ending, speaking after a muted Victory Day parade that lacked military hardware due to fears of Ukrainian attacks. He said he would only meet Zelenskyy once a lasting peace deal is agreed.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe faces defense industry hurdles despite new political will on spending
item.description: European NATO allies are finding that boosting defense spending is easier said than done. Decades of reliance on US equipment and fragmented national defense industries pose major obstacles to achieving military self-sufficiency.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇦🇪 Gulf States Edge Closer to Direct Confrontation with Iran
item.description: Saudi Arabia and UAE have conducted military strikes inside Iran in retaliation for Iranian attacks, marking a shift from proxy conflicts to direct engagement. Kuwait also arrested IRGC members plotting attacks on Bubiyan Island.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days
item.description: The US-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah has been extended by 45 days following Washington talks. Israel carried out fresh strikes even as the extension was announced.

item.title: 🦠 Ebola Outbreak Confirmed in Congo with 65 Deaths
item.description: Africa CDC confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in DR Congo's Ituri province with 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths. The US CDC is monitoring the outbreak and providing technical assistance to both DRC and Uganda.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Pentagon Halts Troop Deployments to Poland and Germany
item.description: The Pentagon is drawing down thousands of troops in Europe by canceling planned deployments to Poland and Germany. The move has drawn bipartisan criticism from US lawmakers concerned about signaling weakness to Russia.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump-Xi Summit Ends With Trade Deals But No Major Breakthroughs
item.description: President Trump concluded his Beijing summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, touting "fantastic trade deals" including Chinese purchases of U.S. oil, but experts say the agreements lack substance and major issues like Taiwan and Iran remain unresolved.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Army Creates Multi-Domain Command-Pacific to Counter China Threats
item.description: The US Army is merging the 7th Infantry Division and 1st Multi-Domain Task Force into a new two-star command for the Indo-Pacific. The command will integrate long-range strike, cyber, electronic warfare, and intelligence capabilities across the region.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Xi Jinping to Make First US State Visit in Over a Decade This Autumn
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi confirmed Xi Jinping will visit the US at Trump's invitation this autumn. Trump said the leaders could meet three more times this year, including at APEC in Shenzhen and G20 in Miami.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Pentagon draws down thousands of troops in Europe
item.description: The Pentagon is reducing US troop levels in Europe by canceling planned deployments to Poland and Germany rather than withdrawing existing forces, according to AP sources. The move comes as the Trump administration reassesses America's military footprint abroad.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil prices surge over 3% as US-Iran standoff persists
item.description: Oil prices jumped more than 3% as comments from President Trump and Iran's foreign minister dented hopes for a deal to end ship attacks and seizures around the Strait of Hormuz. UAE stocks also fell as the deadlock rattled regional markets.
